paper_title: Palynological data of cores MSM5/5–712–2 and PS2863/1–2 from northeastern Fram Strait spanning the last glacial maximum to present

methods.description: Chemical treatment of sediment samples to dissolve carbonate and silica particles, isolating the organic matter fraction (10-106 µm) for palynological analysis.

steps.description: Subject the 10e106 mm fraction was subjected to three hydrochloric acid treatments to dissolve carbonate particles
steps.searchable_summary: Apply hydrochloric acid treatment to sediment fraction

steps.description: interspersed by hydrofluoric acid treatments to dissolve the silica particles.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply hydrofluoric acid treatment to sediment residue
outputs: Sediment residue after HF treatment (organic matter enriched)

steps.description: The residues were mounted on slides in glycerin jelly
steps.searchable_summary: Mount processed sediment residue onto microscope slides

methods.description: Microscopic observation and counting of palynomorphs on prepared slides to obtain raw counts and relative abundances.

steps.description: observed under microscope at 400X optical magnification for palynological analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Observe prepared slides under a microscope

steps.description: Minimum counts of 300 dinocysts per sample were targeted.
steps.searchable_summary: Count palynomorphs, targeting a minimum number of dinocysts
outputs: Raw counts of different palynomorph types per sample

steps.description: Once the counting completed, the palynomorph concentrations in #/g were calculated as follows:
Then, the dinocyst assemblages (relative abundances of taxa in %) in the samples from each site are shown in the following two tables.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate relative abundances of dinocyst taxa
inputs: Raw counts of dinocyst taxa per sample
outputs: Relative abundances (%) of dinocyst taxa per sample

methods.description: Calculation of palynomorph fluxes (#/cm²/yr) based on counted palynomorphs, sample volume, and sedimentation rate.

steps.searchable_summary: Calculate palynomorph concentration per unit volume

steps.description: (2) Flux (#/cm²/yr) ¼ Sedimentation rate (cm/yr) Â Concentration (#/cm³)

methods.description: Quantitative reconstruction of sea-surface conditions (temperature, salinity, sea-ice, productivity) using the Modern Analog Technique (MAT) applied to dinocyst relative abundances.

steps.description: Reconstructions of sea-surface conditions were obtained based on the modern analog technique (MAT; [2]) applied to the dinocyst relative abundances according to Ref. [7].
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain dinocyst relative abundances for samples

steps.description: The MAT approach consists of identifying the 5 best analogs from the dinocyst spectra (relative abundance of each taxa) of the surface sediment data set (modern assemblages) that includes 1776 sites [7,8].
steps.searchable_summary: Identify best modern analogs based on dinocyst spectra
inputs: Modern analog dataset (dinocyst spectra from 1776 sites)
outputs: List of 5 best analog sites from modern dataset for each sample

steps.description: The sea-surface condition values represent the average of the 5 selected analogs weighted inversely to the distance.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate weighted average of sea-surface conditions from analog sites
inputs: Sea-surface condition values for analog sites

steps.description: The maximum and minimum are determined based on the minimum and maximum values from the set of analogs.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine maximum and minimum reconstructed values from analog range
inputs: Sea-surface condition values for the 5 best analog sites
